economic_flags.quote: E-wallet users — disproportionately young, low-income, and first-time digital users — have trust account arrangements of uncertain legal strength
economic_flags.why_biased: Frames the entire e-wallet user base as vulnerable/low-income, ignoring M40/T20 adoption in urban centres for convenience.
economic_flags.fix: Acknowledge that e-wallet adoption spans all income brackets, even if B40 is hit hardest by failures.

gender_flags.quote: Women managing household micro-payments face particular exposure: they are disproportionately targeted by social engineering scams
gender_flags.why_biased: Assigns victimhood to women without citing gender-disaggregated data.
gender_flags.fix: Frame by digital literacy or age unless gender-disaggregated data is cited.

omission_flags: Operator perspective and compliance costs missing
Security features like MFA/facial recognition not acknowledged
evasion_flags: Evades technical complexity of pass-through insurance legislation
shoe_test_failures: Cash risks treated as default state while digital risks treated as policy failure
overall_assessment: Pro-consumer advocacy with heavy economic populism. Factually accurate but tonally hostile toward state and regulators. David vs Goliath framing where B40 is always victim.
